overall.description: Gameplay footage from Horizon Forbidden West showing a character riding a mechanical bird mount through a desert canyon landscape during golden hour. The player discovers a rebel camp location and flies towards a distant mountain fortress, performing smooth aerial maneuvers through the open badlands.

scene_state.functional_zone: desert canyon with rebel camp ruins below

events.description: UI notification appears for newly discovered rebel camp 'Devil's Sweep'

agents.ego_description: piloting a mechanical bird mount through a narrow canyon, banking between red rock walls

entered.detail: tall reddish-orange canyon walls forming the left boundary of the flight path
tall reddish-orange canyon walls forming the right boundary of the flight path
sandy desert floor visible far below the flying mount
ruined structures and debris of the Devil's Sweep rebel camp visible in the canyon below

segments.scene_description: The segment opens with a third-person view following a mechanical bird mount flying through a narrow desert canyon. The camera banks smoothly between towering red rock walls as golden hour sunlight illuminates the scene. A UI notification appears announcing the discovery of the 'Devil's Sweep' rebel camp, with ruined structures visible on the canyon floor below.

scene_state.functional_zone: open desert badlands with scattered rock formations

events.description: mount performs wide banking turn to the left, revealing expansive desert vista

agents.ego_description: performing sweeping aerial maneuvers and wide turns over the open desert expanse

entered.detail: scattered rock outcrops and geological formations emerging from the sand
hazy mountain silhouettes visible on the horizon

segments.scene_description: Exiting the narrow canyon, the scene opens into vast desert badlands under a wide sky with scattered clouds. The mechanical mount executes wide, sweeping turns as it navigates the open airspace, with distant rock formations and mountain ranges visible on the horizon. The desert floor stretches endlessly below with subtle sand dune patterns.

scene_state.functional_zone: desert approach to mountain fortress with strong backlighting

events.description: sun becomes directly visible ahead, creating strong lens flare and backlit conditions

agents.ego_description: flying steadily towards a distant mountain fortress with the sun directly ahead

entered.detail: large fortified structure perched atop a distant mountain peak
bright sun low on the horizon creating golden hour lighting and lens flare effects

segments.scene_description: The final segment shows the approach to a massive mountain fortress silhouetted against the bright sun. Strong golden hour backlighting creates dramatic lens flares as the mechanical bird flies steadily toward the target. The fortress grows from a distant speck to a prominent structure filling the center of the frame, while the surrounding desert canyon terrain passes below.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement that transitions from a confined desert canyon into a vast, open expanse of badlands. It begins by tracking a mechanical bird mount as it banks smoothly between towering red rock walls, with golden hour sunlight illuminating ruined structures on the canyon floor below. As the mount emerges from the narrow passage, the scene opens into a wide sky with scattered clouds and an endless desert floor marked by subtle sand dune patterns. The camera then follows the mount through wide, sweeping turns across the open airspace, finally revealing distant rock formations and mountain ranges stretching across the horizon.

The shot follows a continuous aerial approach toward a massive mountain fortress that gradually dominates the horizon against a brilliant sun. It begins with the fortress as a distant speck, silhouetted by strong golden hour backlighting that casts dramatic lens flares across the frame. As the mechanical bird flies steadily forward, the surrounding desert canyon terrain glides rapidly below, emphasizing the scale of the ascent. The scene then shifts as the structure grows in prominence, expanding from a far-off point to a towering presence that fills the center of the view.
